_comment: Per-object token-normalization map for the golden-file byte-compare harness
(Stage B4). One entry per object type. For each zip member:
tokens           — run-scoped literals embedded in the golden vs the local
run. Both sides are rewritten to the same placeholder
before the byte compare: the golden value 'golden' and
the local value supplied on the compare_fbdi.py command
line ('local' = $PREFIX or $RUN_ID) each become
'placeholder'. 'match' restricts where a token may hit:
whole_field (default) | startswith | substring — so short
numeric ids cannot collide with unrelated business data.
'fields' (optional, 0-based positions) further restricts a
token to the named CSV columns.
date_mask_fields — 0-based CSV field positions (per the object's CTL
column order) that carry generation-time dates
(e.g. DATE_CREATED = NVL(input, SYSDATE)). Masked on
both sides ONLY when both sides match the FBDI date
pattern YYYY/MM/DD — a non-date difference there is
still reported as a real diff.
record_terminator - optional, per member: 'CRLF' when the generator
emits CRLF record endings (supplier family); default
is LF. Declared, never sniffed - a terminator the
file does not actually use fails the round-trip
self-check and is reported as format drift.
Nothing else is normalized. Any other difference is a finding.

format - optional, per member: 'hdl_dat' for HCM Data Loader .dat members (pipe-delimited METADATA/MERGE lines, LF-terminated, NOT quoted CSV). The same token/date-mask machinery applies per field; field 1 is the MERGE/METADATA discriminator. First used by Workers (first HDL golden).

Workers.golden_provenance: Old-stack regression run 116, prefix 9627 (the HCM run behind Workers README 'Known Good/Bad Test Data'; rows are scripts/insert_hcm_regression_data.py in the frozen ConversionTool repo: 2 GOOD workers DMTW001/DMTW002 with all 10 components + 1 BAD DMTW-BAD with only the mandatory 5 and no DOB). This is the first HDL golden: one Worker.dat with 10 pipe-delimited sections (Worker, PersonName, WorkRelationship, WorkTerms, Assignment, PersonEmail, PersonPhone, PersonAddress, PersonNationalIdentifier, PersonLegislativeData). WorkRelationship/WorkTerms/Assignment are auto-generated by the generator from Worker rows (no STG/TFM tables of their own in this object).

Worker.dat._note: The run prefix (golden 9627) prefixes every SourceSystemId / PersonId(SourceSystemId) / PeriodOfServiceId / WorkTermsAssignmentId / AssignmentName / AssignmentNumber value derived from PERSON_NUMBER. startswith on the prefix rewrites all of them on both sides regardless of column position (positions differ per section). Fixed input dates (2026/01/01, DOB 1985/03/15 & 1990/07/22) are identical on both sides, so no date-mask is needed.

Suppliers.golden_provenance: Old-stack regression run 116, prefix 9627. Rows are scripts/insert_regression_test_data.py section 1 (2 GOOD + 1 BAD invalid ORGANIZATION_TYPE; the pre-existing LOADED supplier never transforms so it is not in the file). Generated before the validation-order fix, so the BAD row is in the FBDI by design of that run.

SupplierAddresses.golden_provenance: Old-stack regression run 116, prefix 9627. Rows are insert_regression_test_data.py section 2 (2 GOOD + 1 ghost-supplier BAD). The ghost row is in the golden because run 116 predates the pre-validation ordering fix.

SupplierSites.golden_provenance: Old-stack regression run 116, prefix 9627. Rows are insert_regression_test_data.py section 3 (2 GOOD + 1 ghost-supplier BAD; pre-existing LOADED site excluded). VENDOR_SITE_CODE is PREFIXED(...,15) so '9627RT-SITE-BAD1' truncates to '9627RT-SITE-BAD' -- reproduced, not normalized.

SupplierSiteAssignments.golden_provenance: Old-stack regression run 116, prefix 9627. Rows are insert_regression_test_data.py section 4 (2 GOOD + 1 invalid-BU BAD; pre-existing LOADED assignment excluded). BILL_TO_BU_NAME column is NVL-derived from BUSINESS_UNIT_NAME by the transform.

SupplierContacts.golden_provenance: Old-stack regression run 116, prefix 9627. Rows are insert_regression_test_data.py section 5 (2 GOOD + 1 ghost-supplier BAD).

Customers.golden_provenance: Old-stack regression run 116, prefix 9627. Customers is ONE object; its single zip carries seven HZ CSVs (parties, locations, party sites, party site uses, accounts, account sites, account site uses). Rows are insert_regression_test_data.py sections 6-12 (2 GOOD + 1 BAD per record type). Field 0 of every CSV is BATCH_ID = RUN_ID (116 in the golden). The run prefix (9627) is applied by the transform to the ORIG_SYSTEM_REFERENCE and *_NUMBER fields. Generated before the pre-validation ordering fix, so the BAD rows are in the golden by design of that run. Records are LF-terminated (unlike the CRLF supplier-family CSVs).

GLBalances.golden_provenance: Old-stack run 100000740, prefix 9403, GROUP_ID 100000294 (old stack used a separate group-id sequence; rebuilt stack sets GROUP_ID = RUN_ID per ParameterList)
